text: T_C: a smoothed Burgers bore in u (u_L=1, u_R=0 at x=0, width 0.5) collides with a bright NLS soliton in N (sech^2 at x=-8, amp 1, moving right via phi_x=0.6, kappa=+1). Does the soliton survive to T=8 with N_max>=0.3 and bounded |u|<5? Does ||m||_2(t) decrease (compound-soliton attractor signature)?

Two banks. NLS bank's kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton already encodes the combined fix for EXACTLY this IC (validated u_L=1/u_R=0 + phi_x=0.6 bright soliton at x=-8 to T=8). BKdV bank's kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass + kb-general-firstOrder-Godunov-preShock-baseline confirm the Burgers half of the recipe but say nothing about (N, phi). NLS bank's kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure tells us direct (N, phi) is a dead end -> simplest meaningful baseline is Madelung-Psi. NLS bank's kb-nls-split-linear-phase tells us we MUST split phi=c*x+phi_tilde for c=0.6. NLS bank's kb-nls-sign-convention warns that the standard Madelung-Psi propagator is for STANDARD sign (-Psi_xx/2); the user's literal +sign would be parabolic-unstable, so we adopt the standard sign as a hypothesis (per kb-nls-sign-convention point iii). Progressive plan: E1 = Strang Madelung-Psi + SPECTRAL on u (no MUSCL, no dealias) -> expected Gibbs ringing per kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton. E2 = add MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 on u (single component upgrade). E3 = add 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 and FFT (single further upgrade).

ic: u0=1.0*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (bore u_L=1,u_R=0,w=0.5), N0=1.0*sech^2(x+8) (bright soliton at x=-8 amp 1), phi0=0.6*x
method: Strang Madelung-Psi (standard NLS sign) + SPECTRAL SSP-RK3 on u (no MUSCL, no dealias). Psi=exp(i*0.6*x)*Psi_tilde with Psi_tilde periodic; kinetic operator shifted to -(1/2)*(k+0.6)^2.
cites_bank: kb-nls-direct-n-phi-structural-failure (NLS bank): forces E1 baseline to already use Madelung-Psi rather than direct (N,phi)
kb-nls-strang-splitstep-bright-soliton (NLS bank): Strang is gold-standard primitive
kb-nls-split-linear-phase (NLS bank): required for non-periodic phi
kb-nls-sign-convention (NLS bank): warns + sign is unstable; adopt standard as hypothesis
kb-nls-cfl-split-step (NLS bank): dt<=1e-3 at Nx=256/L=30; use 5e-4
rejects_bank: kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton (NLS bank): the full stacked fix; rejected at E1 per progressive-complexity discipline (cannot skip to full stack)
kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (BKdV bank): would be appropriate for u but rejected at E1 to observe spectral failure mode first

kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs (BKdV bank): negative entry warning against central FD on Burgers; NOT directly rejected here (we use spectral SSP-RK3 which is also non-upwind, so this anti-pattern is acknowledged but the simplest progressive baseline still uses no upwinding)
bank_use_rationale: E1 deliberately uses no MUSCL and no dealias so the failure modes (bore Gibbs ringing, possibly later MI cascade) are observable. Madelung-Psi is included from the start because direct (N,phi) is a known structural dead-end per NLS bank.

diagnostics.blowup_field: u (spectral Burgers, no upwinding -> Gibbs ringing builds to overflow)

interpretation: u sector blew up first (umax 1.0 -> 10 by t=4 -> NaN by t=4.8). Madelung-Psi half remained healthy: mass conserved to 4 sig fig, Nmax bounded at 1.0. This exactly matches kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton's prediction that spectral u Gibbs-rings the bore, and isolates the failure to the u sector. NLS bank entry was predictive; BKdV bank's kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs predicted same anti-pattern. ||m|| growing 3.59 -> 6.65 partly real (off-Mcs IC) but dominated by Gibbs-contaminated u beyond t~3.

rationale: Upgrade u sector to MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 (van-Leer limiter, Godunov flux for f(u)=u^2/2). Single component change per progressive-complexity discipline. Cited by both kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton (NLS bank, dual-fix) and kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (BKdV bank, single confirmation). Keep dt=5e-4, Nx=256.

method: Strang Madelung-Psi + MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 on u (van-Leer limiter, Godunov flux for f(u)=u^2/2). NO dealiasing yet.
cites_bank: kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton (NLS bank): combined recipe validated on EXACTLY this IC to T=8 at Nx=256/dt=5e-4
kb-burgers-MUSCL-Godunov-shock-pass (BKdV bank): MUSCL+Godunov van-Leer baseline for bore (independent confirmation)
kb-general-firstOrder-Godunov-preShock-baseline (BKdV bank): Godunov flux as MUSCL base (rejected first-order, used MUSCL because bore is already a steep front)
kb-nls-split-linear-phase (NLS bank): Galilean factor exp(i*0.6*x) on Psi_tilde
rejects_bank: kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic (NLS bank): defer to E3 per progressive-complexity discipline (one component at a time)
kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls (NLS bank): recommends Nx=512+ on L=60, but task spec mandates Nx=256 on L=30; honor task spec
kb-shallowWater-HLL-dam-break-pass (BKdV bank): HLL flux is alternative but Burgers Godunov is exact and matches NLS bank's specific recipe
bank_use_rationale: Apply the bank-recommended SINGLE component upgrade (MUSCL on u) over E1. NLS bank entry kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton dominates here: it specifies both the Burgers side AND the Madelung side AND the exact dt/Nx that work. BKdV bank's MUSCL-Godunov entry is a redundant confirmation of the Burgers half only.

interpretation: ALL phenomenon targets met: (a) Nmax(T=8)=0.9987 >> 0.3 (soliton survives intact at ~99.9% of initial amplitude); (b) |u_max|=1.0 <<5 (TVD MUSCL produces zero overshoot); (c) bore intact, TV(u)=1.988 matches kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton reported value exactly; (d) ||m||_2(t) DECREASES monotonically from 3.590 to 3.428 — a 4.5% drop over T=8, indicating M_cs surface acts as a (weak) attractor in this run. Mass conserved to 4 sig fig. Soliton peak at x=-3.164 consistent with travel from x=-8 at speed 0.6 to x=-8+0.6*8=-3.2 (transmission through rarefying bore). Spectral tails 1.6e-8 (Psi) and 9.25e-5 (u) both safely below 1e-4 under-resolution flag per kb-nls-resolution-soliton-counting.

rationale: E2 already meets ALL phenomenon targets cleanly. Per session protocol may stop early. However, per kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic the bank-recommended full stack includes 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 — proceeding to E3 as a robustness/verification step: add dealiasing (single further component) and check that the result deviates by <0.2% from E2 (per kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls's recommended verification protocol). If E3 agrees with E2 within tolerance, declare E2 the final result with E3 as a stability/robustness confirmation.

method: Strang Madelung-Psi + MUSCL-Godunov SSP-RK3 on u + 2/3 dealiasing on |Psi|^2 (before pointwise nonlinear) AND on the linear FFT step. Full kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton stack.
cites_bank: kb-nls-23-dealiasing-cubic (NLS bank): apply 2/3 dealias on cubic |Psi|^2*Psi for shock/MI-prone IC
kb-nls-muscl-madelung-bore-soliton (NLS bank): full validated recipe — this E3 matches it exactly
kb-nls-recommended-default-bnls (NLS bank): verification protocol — compare to E2 within 0.2%
kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts (BKdV bank): independent negative confirmation that no-dealiasing on spectral methods leads to spurious peaks/inflation — supports adding dealias
rejects_bank: kb-nls-hard-floor-counterproductive (NLS bank): we are not regularizing N; not applicable here
kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion (BKdV bank): we are using exact Godunov, not LxF; not applicable
bank_use_rationale: E3 is the bank-canonical full stack. Compare to E2 to verify the dealiasing makes <0.2% difference (a robustness check). BKdV bank's no-dealiasing negative entries are CONFIRMATORY here (consistent with NLS bank entry).

interpretation: E3 (dealiased full stack) and E2 (no dealias) agree to within 7e-5 in N (max relative error) — well below the 0.2% tolerance. u-sector is identical (MUSCL-Godunov is unaffected by Psi-side dealiasing). The phi wrap-angle deviation is dominated by atan2 branch wrapping at boundaries and not physically meaningful. Bank-canonical stack matches the minimal MUSCL+Madelung result. The dealiasing here is a SAFETY MARGIN (the spectral tail of Psi was already 1.6e-8 in E2, so dealiasing had little to do). Confirms that for this IC the dual fix (MUSCL on u + Madelung-Psi on Psi) is the controlling component; dealiasing is a no-op-quality robustness add. All phenomenon targets met identically: Nmax(T=8)=0.9987, umax=1.0, bore intact, ||m||_2 decreases 3.590 -> 3.428 (4.5% drop, compound-soliton attractor signature confirmed).

rationale: E3 is the bank-canonical full stack and matches E2 within tolerance, confirming robustness. Declare E3 the final method (preferred for being the bank's stated recipe, with E2 establishing the dealiasing is not strictly necessary for this IC). All phenomenon targets met. Final pred_results/T_C.npy is from E3.
